Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–565, § 1100 Stat. 3181Oct. 27, 1986“This Act [enacting this subchapter] may be known as the ‘Great Basin National Park Act of 1986’.”

children.heading: Purpose; designation
children.label: a Purpose; designation
children.text: In order to preserve for the benefit and inspiration of the people a representative segment of the Great Basin of the Western United States possessing outstanding resources and significant geological and scenic values, there is hereby established the Great Basin National Park (hereinafter in this subchapter referred to as the “park”).

children.heading: Composition; filing of map; public inspection
children.label: b Composition; filing of map; public inspection
children.text: The park shall consist of approximately seventy-six thousand acres, as depicted on the map entitled “Boundary Map, Great Basin National Park, Nevada,” numbered NA–GB 20,017, and dated October 1986. The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the offices of the National Park Service, Department of the Interior, and the Office of the Superintendent, Great Basin National Park, Nevada.

children.heading: Filing of legal description; public inspection
children.label: c Filing of legal description; public inspection
children.text: Within 6 months after , the Secretary of the Interior (hereinafter in this subchapter referred to as the “Secretary”) shall file a legal description of the park designated under this section with the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the United States House of Representatives and with the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the United States Senate. Such legal description shall have the same force and effect as if included in this subchapter, except that the Secretary may correct clerical and typographical errors in such legal description and in the map referred to in subsection (a). The legal description shall be on file and available for public inspection in the offices of the National Park Service, Department of the Interior.October 27, 1986

children.heading: Incorporation of Lehman Caves National Monument within park
children.label: d Incorporation of Lehman Caves National Monument within park

children.text: The Lehman Caves National Monument, designated on , by Presidential proclamation under the authority contained in the Act of () is hereby abolished and the lands incorporated within the Great Basin National Park. Any reference in any law, map, regulation, document, record, or other paper of the United States to such national monument shall be deemed to be a reference to Great Basin National Park.January 24, 1922June 8, 190634 Stat. 2251See References in Text note below.1
Any funds available for purposes of the national monument shall be available for purposes of the park.
